sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच। प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव। न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति।।
transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | prakāśaṃ ca pravṛttiṃ ca moham eva ca pāṇḍava | na dveṣṭi sampravṛttāni na nivṛttāni kāṅkṣati ||
literal_meaning: The Lord said: O Pāṇḍava, he neither hates illumination, activity and delusion when they arise, nor does he long for them when they cease.
contextual_meaning: A psychologically integrated person remains non-reactive to the three guṇas—moments of clarity (sattva), drive (rajas), or confusion (tamas)—neither clinging to pleasant states nor resisting unpleasant ones.
psychological_state: Stable meta-awareness that observes inner states without fusion or avoidance.
trigger_scenario: You notice yourself procrastinating (tamas) and immediately judge it; then recall this verse, breathe, and return to task without self-criticism.
anchor_text: Notice moods like passing weather—no clinging, no resisting.
